name: Stewed Pork with Pickles
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Pat the pork dry with paper towels and sprinkle with desired salt and pepper.
3. Heat oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat on top of the stove.
4. Add the pork, without crowding, and brown well on all sides. You may have to perform this operation in batches.
5. Remove the pieces to a plate as they are brown. Pour off all but about 2 tablespoons fat.
6. Reduce heat to low and replace the pot on the stove.
7. Add the onion and cook for 5 minutes, scraping up any brown bits that cling to the pan.
8. Add the flour and cook another minute, stirring.
9. Add the wine, vinegar, and stock and bring to a boil.
10. Return the pork to the pot with any juices on the plate and add bay leaves and thyme.
11. Cover tightly and transfer the pot to the oven.
12. Cook for 1 ¼ hours, or until the meat is barely tender.
13. Remove the casserole from the oven and, using a slotted spoon, remove the meat from sauce and set aside.
14. Strain the sauce through a fine sieve into a container and discard onions and herbs.
15. Replace the meat in the pot, add the cornichons, mustard, cream, and Brussels sprouts.
16. Cover and replace in oven for 20-to-30 minutes or until meat is tender and sprouts are cooked.
17. Remove from oven and serve with buttered noodles.
